title: 3 reasons for optimism and 3 causes for concern as the Red Sox approach a new season
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Roman Anthony is a promising young talent for the Red Sox, expected to advance his game next season.
Concerns include the setback of young players like Marcelo Mayer and the absence of veteran Alex Bregman.
The World Baseball Classic offers both bonding opportunities and potential injury risks for Red Sox players.

title: Boston police seeking suspect in armed robbery
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Police seek suspect in armed robbery involving multiple victims on Jan. 22 in Dorchester's Adams and Whitten Streets area.
Suspect allegedly approached victims from behind and robbed them at gunpoint in the afternoon incident.
Public urged to provide information via Boston Police detectives at 617-343-4335 or through CrimeStoppers at 1-800-494-TIPS.

title: No license yet for World Cup games as Foxborough asks for public safety funding up front
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Foxborough officials demand $7.8 million upfront for World Cup public safety from the Boston 2026 host committee.
The committee and Kraft Sports promise to cover shortfalls, but payments are proposed post-event, not before.
Foxborough threatens to withhold event licenses over unresolved funding, with a deadline set for March 17.

title: Wu to host first-ever ‘office hours’ for residents to speak with her directly
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Mayor Michelle Wu will host Boston's inaugural 'office hours' event for residents at the Bruce C. Bolling Building in Roxbury.
Residents can discuss city issues directly with Wu and interact with staff from over a dozen city departments.
The event offers city services like voting registration and certificate printing alongside direct communication with city officials.

title: 7 players Bruins could target at 2026 NHL trade deadline
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins are exploring trades before the Friday NHL trade deadline to bolster their roster for the postseason.
Potential targets include Robert Thomas, a center from St. Louis, and Simon Nemec, a defenseman from New Jersey.
Boston is focusing on acquiring players with term, avoiding rentals, to maintain long-term contention prospects.

title: Staff member at special needs residential program in Franklin accused of breaking autistic student’s arm
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Saturs Allison, 27, was indicted for allegedly breaking an autistic student's arm at a Franklin group home.
The incident was allegedly captured on security footage, showing Allison using unauthorized restraint techniques.
Allison was suspended and faces up to five years in prison if convicted; he's due in court April 7.

title: ‘Every run is about defying cancer’: This 18-year-old is running Boston for her childhood friend
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Amanda Lewis, 18, from Charlotte, NC, is running the Boston Marathon with the Dana-Farber Marathon Challenge.
Lewis runs in memory of her friend Lily, who died from brain cancer five years ago at age 13.
Her goal is to raise funds for cancer research and finish the marathon in honor of those affected by cancer.

title: Fall River man sentenced for raping woman when she refused to take naked pictures of young daughter
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Elias Rodrigues Dos Santos Mendes, 55, was sentenced to 8-10 years for rape and child pornography in Fall River.
Dos Santos Mendes pleaded guilty after raping a woman who refused to take nude photos of her 12-year-old daughter.
He will be deported to Brazil upon sentence completion as he is undocumented, said District Attorney Thomas Quinn.

title: What’s next for Patriots’ offense after releasing Stefon Diggs?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Patriots are releasing Stefon Diggs, freeing up $17 million in cap space, and seeking a new WR1.
Alec Pierce and A.J. Brown are potential targets for New England, either through free agency or trade.
The Patriots could also draft a wide receiver at the 31st overall pick to replace Diggs long-term.

title: Each mile is for her miracle: This Granby mom is running the Boston Marathon with her daughter in mind
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Brianna Poehler from Granby, Mass., is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for Miles for Miracles.
Her daughter, a liver transplant survivor, received life-saving care at Boston Children's Hospital at 11 months old.
Poehler aims to raise funds for research and treatments supporting Boston Children's Hospital through her marathon run.
